<!-- generated by `cargo run -p scirust-signal --example vst_benchmark` -->
# VST-selection benchmark (CANR §9)

Reproducible artifact for the CANR "benchmark tool" deliverable
(`CANR_CERTIFIED_ADAPTIVE_REPRESENTATIONS_2026-07-16.md` §9, §12 Phase 1).
Produced by the data-driven VST autotuner (`scirust-signal::denoise::autotune_vst`)
sweeping a grid of **noise model × inner denoiser**: for each cell it selects a
variance-stabilizing transform on a development record and validates it on a
disjoint held-out record. Deterministic (Philox noise, 1024-sample smooth
positive reference, dev seed 1 / held-out seed 2, default VST candidate set).
`gain` is the held-out SNR of the empirically-chosen transform minus direct
(identity-domain) denoising; `beats` marks a positive held-out gain.

Regenerate with:

```text
cargo run -p scirust-signal --example vst_benchmark > docs/research/vst_benchmark_table.md
```

| noise | denoiser | chosen VST | eval SNR (dB) | baseline (dB) | gain (dB) | beats |
|---|---|---|--:|--:|--:|:-:|
| multiplicative | gaussian_smooth | identity | 21.27 | 21.27 | +0.00 | · |
| multiplicative | wiener_global | boxcox(0.50) | 13.83 | 12.99 | +0.85 | ✓ |
| multiplicative | wavelet_soft | identity | 17.29 | 17.29 | +0.00 | · |
| multiplicative | stft_wiener_auto | signed_log | 21.46 | 20.25 | +1.21 | ✓ |
| poisson_like | gaussian_smooth | identity | 26.29 | 26.29 | +0.00 | · |
| poisson_like | wiener_global | anscombe | 20.26 | 19.76 | +0.50 | ✓ |
| poisson_like | wavelet_soft | identity | 19.21 | 19.21 | +0.00 | · |
| poisson_like | stft_wiener_auto | anscombe | 29.55 | 28.73 | +0.82 | ✓ |
| additive_gaussian | gaussian_smooth | identity | 35.88 | 35.88 | +0.00 | · |
| additive_gaussian | wiener_global | identity | 30.12 | 30.12 | +0.00 | · |
| additive_gaussian | wavelet_soft | anscombe | 23.88 | 24.11 | -0.23 | · |
| additive_gaussian | stft_wiener_auto | identity | 38.04 | 38.04 | +0.00 | · |

## Reading the table (the honest verdict)

- **A VST pays off only for signal-dependent noise fed to a denoiser that
  assumes homoscedastic noise.** The `wiener_global` and `stft_wiener_auto`
  rows on `multiplicative` and `poisson_like` are the four positive cells
  (+0.50 to +1.21 dB), and the autotuner picks the *matched* transform from
  data — Box–Cox / signed-log for multiplicative (σ ∝ level), Anscombe for
  Poisson-like (σ = √level). `stft_wiener_auto` is the largest beneficiary,
  matching this crate's `vst_denoise_auto` design note.
- **A linear smoother is never helped.** Every `gaussian_smooth` cell chooses
  Identity with exactly 0 dB gain: a linear filter of a smooth signal averages
  zero-mean noise regardless of heteroscedasticity, so there is nothing for a
  VST to fix (consistent with the ATRA/CANR finding).
- **Raw-MAD wavelet thresholding is not helped either**, on either
  signal-dependent model — reproducing this crate's documented VisuShrink
  negative result (its mid-range MAD threshold already acts as an accidental
  level-adaptive threshold).
- **On homoscedastic noise no VST wins**; the one non-Identity pick
  (`wavelet_soft`) *loses* on held-out (−0.23 dB) and is correctly reported as
  not beating the baseline — the pre-registered kill signal (CANR §13), left in
  the table rather than hidden.

The negatives are the point: the tool tells you *when not* to stabilize, with a
held-out number, instead of applying a VST on faith.
